origin	git@github.com:patflynn/cosmo.git (push)
The file /tmp/klaus-sessions/cosmo/20260429-2156-e94e/modules/common/system.nix has been updated successfully.

lines: # Let systemd-oomd kill runaway system services before the kernel's own OOM
# path leaves user sessions stuck waiting on swap-in.
systemd.oomd = {
-      enable = true;
-      enableSystemSlice = true;
+      enable = lib.mkDefault true;
+      enableSystemSlice = lib.mkDefault true;
-    # Backstop: hard memory ceiling on nix-daemon so a single build can't eat
-    # the whole machine during the nightly auto-upgrade window.
-    systemd.services.nix-daemon.serviceConfig.MemoryHigh = lib.mkDefault "20G";
+    # Backstop: cap nix-daemon at a fraction of host RAM so a single build can't
+    # eat the whole machine during the nightly auto-upgrade window. Percentage
+    # keeps this portable across hosts with very different memory sizes.
+    systemd.services.nix-daemon.serviceConfig.MemoryHigh = lib.mkDefault "80%";

content.content: The file /tmp/klaus-sessions/cosmo/20260429-2156-e94e/modules/common/system.nix has been updated successfully.

lines: boot.kernel.sysctl."vm.swappiness" = lib.mkDefault 10;
# Let systemd-oomd kill runaway system services before the kernel's own OOM
-    # path leaves user sessions stuck waiting on swap-in.
+    # path leaves user sessions stuck waiting on swap-in. `enable` skips
+    # mkDefault on purpose: nixos-wsl ships `oomd.enable = lib.mkDefault false`
+    # for older WSL kernels lacking PSI, and two mkDefaults would collide.
+    # Modern WSL2 kernels do support oomd, so we override to true everywhere.
systemd.oomd = {
-      enable = lib.mkDefault true;
+      enable = true;
enableSystemSlice = lib.mkDefault true;

content.text: All flake checks pass. Now let me commit and push.

input.command:
git add modules/common/system.nix && git commit -m "modules/common/system: portable OOMD/MemoryHigh defaults

- Use a percentage (80%) for nix-daemon MemoryHigh so the cap scales
  with host RAM instead of being meaningfully different on a 16G laptop
  vs a 64G workstation.
- Make systemd.oomd.enableSystemSlice a mkDefault so hosts can opt out.
- Leave oomd.enable as a hard true and document why: nixos-wsl already
  sets it to lib.mkDefault false for old WSL kernels, so two mkDefaults
  would collide. Modern WSL2 supports oomd, so we want it on globally." && git push

content.text: Now let me reply to both review comments.

input.command: gh api repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/515/comments/3165225035/replies -f body='Adopted for `enableSystemSlice` (now `lib.mkDefault true`). Kept `enable = true` without `mkDefault` because nixos-wsl already declares `systemd.oomd.enable = lib.mkDefault false` for older WSL kernels — two `mkDefault`s collide and break evaluation. Added a comment explaining the asymmetry.'

gh api repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/515/comments/3165225039/replies -f body='Adopted: changed `MemoryHigh` to `lib.mkDefault "80%"` so the cap scales with host RAM rather than being meaningless on small hosts and over-permissive on large ones.'

content.text:
Both review comments addressed and replied to. Pushed commit `5c00ac7`:

- **`MemoryHigh "80%"`**: adopted as suggested — scales with host RAM.
- **`enableSystemSlice = lib.mkDefault true`**: adopted.
- **`enable = true`**: kept hard-set (with explanatory comment) because nixos-wsl already has `lib.mkDefault false` for it; using `mkDefault` would create a priority collision and break flake evaluation on `makers-nix`.

`nix flake check` passes for all hosts.

result:
Both review comments addressed and replied to. Pushed commit `5c00ac7`:

- **`MemoryHigh "80%"`**: adopted as suggested — scales with host RAM.
- **`enableSystemSlice = lib.mkDefault true`**: adopted.
- **`enable = true`**: kept hard-set (with explanatory comment) because nixos-wsl already has `lib.mkDefault false` for it; using `mkDefault` would create a priority collision and break flake evaluation on `makers-nix`.

`nix flake check` passes for all hosts.
